en.summary: The Prophet (SAWW), as narrated by Imam al-Baqir (AS), lists five unique distinctions given to him by Allah. These are foundational to Islamic law and theology: the universality of prayer locations, the permissibility of war spoils, divine support through fear cast into enemies' hearts, the gift of concise yet comprehensive speech, and the power of intercession on the Day of Judgment.

key_terms.جوامع الكلم: jawami' al-kalim - comprehensive speech: the ability to express vast meanings in few words
key_terms.الشفاعة: al-shafa'a - intercession on the Day of Judgment
key_terms.المغنم: al-maghnam - spoils/booty of war, made lawful for the Prophet's nation

en.seo_question: What five things were given uniquely to Prophet Muhammad?

ur.summary: نبی اکرم صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ نے پانچ منفرد عطایا بیان فرمائے۔

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أَبَانٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الْجُعْفِيِّ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ الْبَاقِرَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يَقُولُ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: أُعْطِيتُ خَمْسًا لَمْ يُعْطَهَا أَحَدٌ قَبْلِي جُعِلَتْ لِيَ الْأَرْضُ مَسْجِدًا وَطَهُورًا وَأُحِلَّ لِيَ الْمَغْنَمُ وَنُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ وَأُعْطِيتُ جَوَامِعَ الْكَلِمِ وَأُعْطِيتُ الشَّفَاعَةَ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid, al-Saduq's teacher
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid al-Qummi (d. 343 AH)
al-Husayn ibn al-Hasan ibn Aban, hadith narrator
al-Husayn ibn Sa'id al-Ahwazi (3rd century AH), prolific hadith compiler
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr al-Azdi (d. 217 AH), one of the most reliable Shia hadith narrators
Hammad ibn Uthman (d. ~190 AH), reliable companion of Imams al-Sadiq and al-Kazim
Isma'il ibn Jabir al-Ju'fi, companion of Imam al-Baqir
Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam (d. 114 AH)

similar_content_hints.description: The famous hadith 'I was given jawami' al-kalim' appears in multiple Sunni and Shia collections including Sahih al-Bukhari and al-Kafi.
Narrations about the Prophet's five unique gifts also appear in Sahih Muslim and other major collections.
